mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.2pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when both series rise concurrently, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher equity valuations over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day SPX returns when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4%, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls directly increase aggregate wage income and consumer spending capacity, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 20-day SPX direction, with a cumulative rank correlation below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.35% monthly threshold forces markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.35%, invalidating the inflation-fear transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly industrial production confirms synchronized demand and supply expansion, reducing recession probabilities and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and INDPRO signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in over 55% of synchronized expansion quarters, with a decayed information coefficient below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with a stable or rising unemployment rate indicate policy-induced economic cooling that suppresses corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising rates and non-falling unemployment fails to predict negative 20-day SPX returns, with a hit rate below 45% across all tested tightening episodes.
